component.name: Whatsie
component.summary: Feature-rich WhatsApp Web client for the Linux desktop

description.p: Whatsie is a native Linux desktop wrapper for WhatsApp Web, built on Qt and QtWebEngine. It brings WhatsApp out of the browser tab and into a proper desktop application with system integration, privacy controls, and customization options unavailable in a plain browser.

ul.li: Light and Dark themes with automatic OS-level switching
Custom in-app notification popups and native system notifications
Built-in download manager
Spell checker with support for 31 languages
App lock with PIN protection and configurable auto-lock timeout
Global keyboard shortcuts
Per-session audio mute and notification suppression
Do-not-disturb mode
Configurable notification popup timeout
Minimize to system tray on start or window close
Custom user-agent string and zoom control

screenshot.caption: Hardware access controls and keyboard shortcuts

description.p: Minor release focused on build-system and runtime stability improvements:

ul.li: Migrated primary build workflow to CMake + Ninja for Qt 6 development
Refined Qt 6 WebEngine integration and permission handling paths
Improved desktop notification delivery by using org.freedesktop.Notifications directly on Qt 6
Improved session persistence behavior via consistent profile handling
Updated in-app theme application logic for current WhatsApp Web changes

description.p: Major release — Qt 6 port with significant improvements:

ul.li: Ported to Qt 6 and QtWebEngine 6 for better performance and long-term support
Reliable light/dark theme toggle without requiring a page reload
Persistent named WebEngine profile with proper cookie and storage handling
Improved permission handling for camera, microphone, and notifications
Centralised profile management and refactored codebase
